"""Command to list plans with filtering."""

from collections.abc import Callable
from typing import ParamSpec, TypeVar

import click
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text

from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.display_utils import strip_rich_markup
from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir
from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
from erk.tui.data.types import PlanFilters, PlanRowData
from erk.tui.sorting.logic import sort_plans
from erk.tui.sorting.types import SortKey, SortState
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.fake import FakeBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.real import RealBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.fake import FakeClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.real import RealClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import get_pr_status_emoji
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, GitHubRepoLocation, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.http.auth import fetch_github_token
from erk_shared.gateway.http.fake import FakeHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.http.real import RealHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.real import RealPlanDataProvider
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output

P = ParamSpec("P")
T = TypeVar("T")


def format_pr_cell(pr: PullRequestInfo, *, use_graphite: bool, graphite_url: str | None) -> str:
    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗

    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.

    Args:
        pr: PR information
        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)

    Returns:
        Formatted string for table cell with OSC 8 hyperlink
    """
    emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(pr)
    pr_text = f"#{pr.number}"

    # Append 🔗 for PRs that will close the issue when merged
    if pr.will_close_target:
        emoji += "🔗"

    # Determine which URL to use
    url = graphite_url if use_graphite else pr.url

    # Make PR number clickable if URL is available
    # Rich supports OSC 8 via [link=...] markup
    if url:
        return f"[link={url}]{pr_text}[/link] {emoji}"
    else:
        return f"{pr_text} {emoji}"


def plan_filter_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """Shared filter options for plan list commands."""
    f = click.option(
        "--label",
        multiple=True,
        help="Filter by label (can be specified multiple times for AND logic)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--state",
        type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"], case_sensitive=False),
        help="Filter by state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--run-state",
        type=click.Choice(
            ["queued", "in_progress", "success", "failure", "cancelled"], case_sensitive=False
        ),
        help="Filter by workflow run state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--limit",
        type=int,
        help="Maximum number of results to return",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--all-users",
        "-A",
        is_flag=True,
        default=False,
        help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--sort",
        type=click.Choice(["issue", "activity"], case_sensitive=False),
        default="issue",
        help="Sort order: by issue number (default) or recent branch activity",
    )(f)
    return f


def dash_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """TUI-specific options for dash command."""
    f = click.option(
        "--interval",
        type=float,
        default=15.0,
        help="Refresh interval in seconds (default: 15.0)",
    )(f)
    return f


def _build_static_table(
    rows: list[PlanRowData],
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> Table:
    """Build a Rich static table from PlanRowData rows.

    Column order matches the TUI PlanDataTable exactly.

    Args:
        rows: List of PlanRowData to display
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Rich Table populated with rows
    """
    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")

    # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns()
    table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6)
    table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4)
    table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7)
    table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5)
    table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42)
    table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9)
    if show_pr_column:
        table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5)

    for row in rows:
        values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column)
        table.add_row(*values)

    return table


def _row_to_static_values(
    row: PlanRowData,
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
    """Convert PlanRowData to static table cell values.

    Mirrors PlanDataTable._row_to_values() for Rich Console rendering.

    Args:
        row: Plan row data
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Tuple of cell values matching column order
    """
    # Format plan/PR number
    plan_cell: str | Text = f"#{row.plan_id}"
    if row.plan_url:
        plan_cell = f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"

    # Format objective cell
    objective_cell: str | Text = row.objective_display
    if row.objective_issue is not None and row.objective_url is not None:
        objective_cell = f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"
    elif row.objective_issue is not None:
        objective_cell = Text(row.objective_display, style="cyan")

    # Compact location emoji
    location_parts: list[str] = []
    if row.exists_locally:
        location_parts.append("\U0001f4bb")
    if row.run_url is not None:
        location_parts.append("\u2601")
    location_cell = "".join(location_parts) if location_parts else "-"

    # run-id and run-state
    run_id: str | Text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_id_display)
    if row.run_url:
        run_id = f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"
    run_state_text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_state_display)
    run_state_emoji = run_state_text.split(" ", 1)[0] if run_state_text.strip() else "-"

    # Build values list matching column order
    stage_display = strip_rich_markup(row.lifecycle_display)
    values: list[str | Text] = [
        plan_cell,
        stage_display,
        row.status_display,
        row.created_display,
        objective_cell,
        location_cell,
        row.pr_head_branch or row.worktree_branch or "-",
        run_id,
        run_state_emoji,
    ]
    values.append(row.author)

    checks_display = strip_rich_markup(row.checks_display)
    comments_display = strip_rich_markup(row.comments_display)
    if show_pr_column:
        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
        if row.pr_url:
            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
    else:
        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
    return tuple(values)


def _list_plans_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Implementation logic for listing plans with optional filters.

    Uses RealPlanDataProvider to fetch data (same as erk dash TUI),
    then renders as a static Rich table matching the TUI column layout.
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Construct RealPlanDataProvider
    # Only fetch_plans() and fetch_branch_activity() are used here;
    # neither requires clipboard, browser, or http_client, so use fakes.
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=FakeClipboard(),
        browser=FakeBrowserLauncher(),
        http_client=FakeHttpClient(),
    )

    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=True,
        show_runs=True,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Fetch data via provider
    rows = provider.fetch_plans(filters)

    if not rows:
        user_output("No plans found matching the criteria.")
        return

    # Sort rows
    sort_key = SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID
    if sort_key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:
        activity_by_plan = provider.fetch_branch_activity(rows)
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key, activity_by_plan=activity_by_plan)
    else:
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key)

    # Build and display static table
    table = _build_static_table(
        rows,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    user_output(f"\nFound {len(rows)} plan(s):\n")
    console = Console(stderr=True, width=200, force_terminal=True)
    console.print(table)
    console.print()


def _run_interactive_mode(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    runs: bool,
    prs: bool,
    limit: int | None,
    interval: float,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Run interactive TUI mode.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
        label: Labels to filter by
        state: State filter ("open", "closed", or None)
        run_state: Workflow run state filter
        runs: Whether to show run columns
        prs: Whether to show PR columns
        limit: Maximum number of results
        interval: Refresh interval in seconds
        all_users: If True, show plans from all users; if False, filter to authenticated user
        sort: Sort order ("issue" or "activity")
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    # Get owner/repo from RepoContext (already populated via git remote URL parsing)
    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter: None for all users, authenticated username otherwise
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Create data provider and filters
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))
    clipboard = RealClipboard()
    browser = RealBrowserLauncher()

    # Fetch GitHub token once at startup for fast HTTP client
    token = fetch_github_token()
    http_client = RealHttpClient(token=token, base_url="https://api.github.com")

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=clipboard,
        browser=browser,
        http_client=http_client,
    )
    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=prs,
        show_runs=runs,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Convert sort string to SortState
    initial_sort = SortState(key=SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID)

    # Run the TUI app
    app = ErkDashApp(
        provider=provider,
        filters=filters,
        refresh_interval=interval,
        initial_sort=initial_sort,
        plan_backend="planned_pr",
    )
    app.run()


@click.command("list")
@plan_filter_options
@click.pass_obj
def list_plans(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """List plans as a static table.

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Examples:
        erk plan list                    # Your plans only
        erk plan list --all-users        # All users' plans
        erk plan list -A                 # All users' plans (short form)
        erk plan list --state open
        erk plan list --label erk-plan --label bug
        erk plan list --limit 10
        erk plan list --run-state in_progress
        erk plan list --sort activity    # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    _list_plans_impl(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )


@click.command("dash")
@plan_filter_options
@dash_options
@click.pass_obj
def dash(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
    interval: float,
) -> None:
    """Interactive plan dashboard (TUI).

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Launches an interactive terminal UI for viewing and managing plans.
    Shows all columns (runs) by default. For a static table output, use
    'erk plan list' instead.

    Examples:
        erk dash                         # Your plans only
        erk dash --all-users             # All users' plans
        erk dash -A                      # All users' plans (short form)
        erk dash --interval 10
        erk dash --label erk-plan --state open
        erk dash --limit 10
        erk dash --run-state in_progress
        erk dash --sort activity         # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    # Default to showing all columns (runs=True)
    prs = True  # Always show PRs
    runs = True  # Default to showing runs

    _run_interactive_mode(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        runs=runs,
        prs=prs,
        limit=limit,
        interval=interval,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )

from collections.abc import Callable
from typing import ParamSpec, TypeVar

import click
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text

from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.display_utils import strip_rich_markup
from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir
from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
from erk.tui.data.types import PlanFilters, PlanRowData
from erk.tui.sorting.logic import sort_plans
from erk.tui.sorting.types import SortKey, SortState
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.fake import FakeBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.real import RealBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.fake import FakeClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.real import RealClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import get_pr_status_emoji
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, GitHubRepoLocation, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.http.auth import fetch_github_token
from erk_shared.gateway.http.fake import FakeHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.http.real import RealHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.real import RealPlanDataProvider
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output

P = ParamSpec("P")
T = TypeVar("T")


def format_pr_cell(pr: PullRequestInfo, *, use_graphite: bool, graphite_url: str | None) -> str:
    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗

    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.

    Args:
        pr: PR information
        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)

    Returns:
        Formatted string for table cell with OSC 8 hyperlink
    """
    emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(pr)
    pr_text = f"#{pr.number}"

    # Append 🔗 for PRs that will close the issue when merged
    if pr.will_close_target:
        emoji += "🔗"

    # Determine which URL to use
    url = graphite_url if use_graphite else pr.url

    # Make PR number clickable if URL is available
    # Rich supports OSC 8 via [link=...] markup
    if url:
        return f"[link={url}]{pr_text}[/link] {emoji}"
    else:
        return f"{pr_text} {emoji}"


def plan_filter_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """Shared filter options for plan list commands."""
    f = click.option(
        "--label",
        multiple=True,
        help="Filter by label (can be specified multiple times for AND logic)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--state",
        type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"], case_sensitive=False),
        help="Filter by state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--run-state",
        type=click.Choice(
            ["queued", "in_progress", "success", "failure", "cancelled"], case_sensitive=False
        ),
        help="Filter by workflow run state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--limit",
        type=int,
        help="Maximum number of results to return",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--all-users",
        "-A",
        is_flag=True,
        default=False,
        help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--sort",
        type=click.Choice(["issue", "activity"], case_sensitive=False),
        default="issue",
        help="Sort order: by issue number (default) or recent branch activity",
    )(f)
    return f


def dash_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """TUI-specific options for dash command."""
    f = click.option(
        "--interval",
        type=float,
        default=15.0,
        help="Refresh interval in seconds (default: 15.0)",
    )(f)
    return f


def _build_static_table(
    rows: list[PlanRowData],
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> Table:
    """Build a Rich static table from PlanRowData rows.

    Column order matches the TUI PlanDataTable exactly.

    Args:
        rows: List of PlanRowData to display
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Rich Table populated with rows
    """
    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")

    # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns()
    table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6)
    table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4)
    table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7)
    table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5)
    table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42)
    table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9)
    if show_pr_column:
        table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5)

    for row in rows:
        values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column)
        table.add_row(*values)

    return table


def _row_to_static_values(
    row: PlanRowData,
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
    """Convert PlanRowData to static table cell values.

    Mirrors PlanDataTable._row_to_values() for Rich Console rendering.

    Args:
        row: Plan row data
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Tuple of cell values matching column order
    """
    # Format plan/PR number
    plan_cell: str | Text = f"#{row.plan_id}"
    if row.plan_url:
        plan_cell = f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"

    # Format objective cell
    objective_cell: str | Text = row.objective_display
    if row.objective_issue is not None and row.objective_url is not None:
        objective_cell = f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"
    elif row.objective_issue is not None:
        objective_cell = Text(row.objective_display, style="cyan")

    # Compact location emoji
    location_parts: list[str] = []
    if row.exists_locally:
        location_parts.append("\U0001f4bb")
    if row.run_url is not None:
        location_parts.append("\u2601")
    location_cell = "".join(location_parts) if location_parts else "-"

    # run-id and run-state
    run_id: str | Text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_id_display)
    if row.run_url:
        run_id = f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"
    run_state_text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_state_display)
    run_state_emoji = run_state_text.split(" ", 1)[0] if run_state_text.strip() else "-"

    # Build values list matching column order
    stage_display = strip_rich_markup(row.lifecycle_display)
    values: list[str | Text] = [
        plan_cell,
        stage_display,
        row.status_display,
        row.created_display,
        objective_cell,
        location_cell,
        row.pr_head_branch or row.worktree_branch or "-",
        run_id,
        run_state_emoji,
    ]
    values.append(row.author)

    checks_display = strip_rich_markup(row.checks_display)
    comments_display = strip_rich_markup(row.comments_display)
    if show_pr_column:
        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
        if row.pr_url:
            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
    else:
        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
    return tuple(values)


def _list_plans_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Implementation logic for listing plans with optional filters.

    Uses RealPlanDataProvider to fetch data (same as erk dash TUI),
    then renders as a static Rich table matching the TUI column layout.
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Construct RealPlanDataProvider
    # Only fetch_plans() and fetch_branch_activity() are used here;
    # neither requires clipboard, browser, or http_client, so use fakes.
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=FakeClipboard(),
        browser=FakeBrowserLauncher(),
        http_client=FakeHttpClient(),
    )

    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=True,
        show_runs=True,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Fetch data via provider
    rows = provider.fetch_plans(filters)

    if not rows:
        user_output("No plans found matching the criteria.")
        return

    # Sort rows
    sort_key = SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID
    if sort_key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:
        activity_by_plan = provider.fetch_branch_activity(rows)
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key, activity_by_plan=activity_by_plan)
    else:
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key)

    # Build and display static table
    table = _build_static_table(
        rows,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    user_output(f"\nFound {len(rows)} plan(s):\n")
    console = Console(stderr=True, width=200, force_terminal=True)
    console.print(table)
    console.print()


def _run_interactive_mode(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    runs: bool,
    prs: bool,
    limit: int | None,
    interval: float,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Run interactive TUI mode.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
        label: Labels to filter by
        state: State filter ("open", "closed", or None)
        run_state: Workflow run state filter
        runs: Whether to show run columns
        prs: Whether to show PR columns
        limit: Maximum number of results
        interval: Refresh interval in seconds
        all_users: If True, show plans from all users; if False, filter to authenticated user
        sort: Sort order ("issue" or "activity")
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    # Get owner/repo from RepoContext (already populated via git remote URL parsing)
    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter: None for all users, authenticated username otherwise
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Create data provider and filters
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))
    clipboard = RealClipboard()
    browser = RealBrowserLauncher()

    # Fetch GitHub token once at startup for fast HTTP client
    token = fetch_github_token()
    http_client = RealHttpClient(token=token, base_url="https://api.github.com")

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=clipboard,
        browser=browser,
        http_client=http_client,
    )
    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=prs,
        show_runs=runs,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Convert sort string to SortState
    initial_sort = SortState(key=SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID)

    # Run the TUI app
    app = ErkDashApp(
        provider=provider,
        filters=filters,
        refresh_interval=interval,
        initial_sort=initial_sort,
        plan_backend="planned_pr",
    )
    app.run()


@click.command("list")
@plan_filter_options
@click.pass_obj
def list_plans(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """List plans as a static table.

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Examples:
        erk plan list                    # Your plans only
        erk plan list --all-users        # All users' plans
        erk plan list -A                 # All users' plans (short form)
        erk plan list --state open
        erk plan list --label erk-plan --label bug
        erk plan list --limit 10
        erk plan list --run-state in_progress
        erk plan list --sort activity    # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    _list_plans_impl(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )


@click.command("dash")
@plan_filter_options
@dash_options
@click.pass_obj
def dash(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
    interval: float,
) -> None:
    """Interactive plan dashboard (TUI).

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Launches an interactive terminal UI for viewing and managing plans.
    Shows all columns (runs) by default. For a static table output, use
    'erk plan list' instead.

    Examples:
        erk dash                         # Your plans only
        erk dash --all-users             # All users' plans
        erk dash -A                      # All users' plans (short form)
        erk dash --interval 10
        erk dash --label erk-plan --state open
        erk dash --limit 10
        erk dash --run-state in_progress
        erk dash --sort activity         # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    # Default to showing all columns (runs=True)
    prs = True  # Always show PRs
    runs = True  # Default to showing runs

    _run_interactive_mode(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        runs=runs,
        prs=prs,
        limit=limit,
        interval=interval,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )

    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗

    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.

    Args:
        pr: PR information
        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)

    Returns:

    f = click.option(

        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
        if row.pr_url:
            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
    else:
        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
    return tuple(values)


def _list_plans_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Implementation logic for listing plans with optional filters.

    Uses RealPlanDataProvider to fetch data (same as erk dash TUI),
    then renders as a static Rich table matching the TUI column layout.
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Construct RealPlanDataProvider
    # Only fetch_plans() and fetch_branch_activity() are used here;
    # neither requires clipboard, browser, or http_client, so use fakes.
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=FakeClipboard(),
        browser=FakeBrowserLauncher(),
        http_client=FakeHttpClient(),
    )

    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=True,
        show_runs=True,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Fetch data via provider
    rows = provider.fetch_plans(filters)

    if not rows:
        user_output("No plans found matching the criteria.")
        return

    # Sort rows
    sort_key = SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID
    if sort_key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:
        activity_by_plan = provider.fetch_branch_activity(rows)
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key, activity_by_plan=activity_by_plan)
    else:
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key)

    # Build and display static table
    table = _build_static_table(
        rows,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    user_output(f"\nFound {len(rows)} plan(s):\n")
    console = Console(stderr=True, width=200, force_terminal=True)
    console.print(table)
    console.print()


def _run_interactive_mode(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    runs: bool,
    prs: bool,
    limit: int | None,
    interval: float,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Run interactive TUI mode.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
        label: Labels to filter by
        state: State filter ("open", "closed", or None)
        run_state: Workflow run state filter
        runs: Whether to show run columns
        prs: Whether to show PR columns
        limit: Maximum number of results
        interval: Refresh interval in seconds
        all_users: If True, show plans from all users; if False, filter to authenticated user
        sort: Sort order ("issue" or "activity")
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    # Get owner/repo from RepoContext (already populated via git remote URL parsing)
    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter: None for all users, authenticated username otherwise
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Create data provider and filters
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))
    clipboard = RealClipboard()
    browser = RealBrowserLauncher()

    # Fetch GitHub token once at startup for fast HTTP client
    token = fetch_github_token()
    http_client = RealHttpClient(token=token, base_url="https://api.github.com")

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=clipboard,
        browser=browser,
        http_client=http_client,
    )
    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=prs,
        show_runs=runs,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Convert sort string to SortState
    initial_sort = SortState(key=SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID)

    # Run the TUI app
    app = ErkDashApp(
        provider=provider,
        filters=filters,
        refresh_interval=interval,
        initial_sort=initial_sort,
        plan_backend="planned_pr",
    )
    app.run()


@click.command("list")
@plan_filter_options
@click.pass_obj
def list_plans(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """List plans as a static table.

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Examples:
        erk plan list                    # Your plans only
        erk plan list --all-users        # All users' plans
        erk plan list -A                 # All users' plans (short form)
        erk plan list --state open
        erk plan list --label erk-plan --label bug
        erk plan list --limit 10
        erk plan list --run-state in_progress
        erk plan list --sort activity    # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    _list_plans_impl(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )


@click.command("dash")
@plan_filter_options
@dash_options
@click.pass_obj
def dash(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
    interval: float,
) -> None:
    """Interactive plan dashboard (TUI).

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Launches an interactive terminal UI for viewing and managing plans.
    Shows all columns (runs) by default. For a static table output, use
    'erk plan list' instead.

    Examples:

        erk dash --all-users             # All users' plans
        erk dash -A                      # All users' plans (short form)
        erk dash --interval 10
        erk dash --label erk-plan --state open
        erk dash --limit 10
        erk dash --run-state in_progress
        erk dash --sort activity         # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    # Default to showing all columns (runs=True)
    prs = True  # Always show PRs
    runs = True  # Default to showing runs

    _run_interactive_mode(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        runs=runs,
        prs=prs,
        limit=limit,
        interval=interval,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )

"""Command to list plans with filtering."""

from collections.abc import Callable
from typing import ParamSpec, TypeVar

import click
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text

from erk.cli.commands.plan.output_builders import build_plan_list_entry
from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.cli.output.generic_renderer import render_json_list
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.display_utils import strip_rich_markup
from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir
from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
from erk.tui.data.types import PlanFilters, PlanRowData
from erk.tui.sorting.logic import sort_plans
from erk.tui.sorting.types import SortKey, SortState
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.fake import FakeBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.real import RealBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.fake import FakeClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.real import RealClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import get_pr_status_emoji
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, GitHubRepoLocation, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.http.auth import fetch_github_token
from erk_shared.gateway.http.fake import FakeHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.http.real import RealHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.real import RealPlanDataProvider
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output

P = ParamSpec("P")
T = TypeVar("T")


def format_pr_cell(pr: PullRequestInfo, *, use_graphite: bool, graphite_url: str | None) -> str:
    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗

    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.

    Args:
        pr: PR information
        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)

    Returns:
        Formatted string for table cell with OSC 8 hyperlink
    """
    emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(pr)
    pr_text = f"#{pr.number}"

    # Append 🔗 for PRs that will close the issue when merged
    if pr.will_close_target:
        emoji += "🔗"

    # Determine which URL to use
    url = graphite_url if use_graphite else pr.url

    # Make PR number clickable if URL is available
    # Rich supports OSC 8 via [link=...] markup
    if url:
        return f"[link={url}]{pr_text}[/link] {emoji}"
    else:
        return f"{pr_text} {emoji}"


def plan_filter_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """Shared filter options for plan list commands."""
    f = click.option(
        "--label",
        multiple=True,
        help="Filter by label (can be specified multiple times for AND logic)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--state",
        type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"], case_sensitive=False),
        help="Filter by state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--run-state",
        type=click.Choice(
            ["queued", "in_progress", "success", "failure", "cancelled"], case_sensitive=False
        ),
        help="Filter by workflow run state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--limit",
        type=int,
        help="Maximum number of results to return",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--all-users",
        "-A",
        is_flag=True,
        default=False,
        help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--sort",
        type=click.Choice(["issue", "activity"], case_sensitive=False),
        default="issue",
        help="Sort order: by issue number (default) or recent branch activity",
    )(f)
    return f


def dash_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """TUI-specific options for dash command."""
    f = click.option(
        "--interval",
        type=float,
        default=15.0,
        help="Refresh interval in seconds (default: 15.0)",
    )(f)
    return f


def _build_static_table(
    rows: list[PlanRowData],
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> Table:
    """Build a Rich static table from PlanRowData rows.

    Column order matches the TUI PlanDataTable exactly.

    Args:
        rows: List of PlanRowData to display
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Rich Table populated with rows
    """
    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")

    # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns()
    table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6)
    table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4)
    table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7)
    table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5)
    table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42)
    table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9)
    if show_pr_column:
        table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5)

    for row in rows:
        values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column)
        table.add_row(*values)

    return table


def _row_to_static_values(
    row: PlanRowData,
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
    """Convert PlanRowData to static table cell values.

    Mirrors PlanDataTable._row_to_values() for Rich Console rendering.

    Args:
        row: Plan row data
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Tuple of cell values matching column order
    """
    # Format plan/PR number
    plan_cell: str | Text = f"#{row.plan_id}"
    if row.plan_url:
        plan_cell = f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"

    # Format objective cell
    objective_cell: str | Text = row.objective_display
    if row.objective_issue is not None and row.objective_url is not None:
        objective_cell = f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"
    elif row.objective_issue is not None:
        objective_cell = Text(row.objective_display, style="cyan")

    # Compact location emoji
    location_parts: list[str] = []
    if row.exists_locally:
        location_parts.append("\U0001f4bb")
    if row.run_url is not None:
        location_parts.append("\u2601")
    location_cell = "".join(location_parts) if location_parts else "-"

    # run-id and run-state
    run_id: str | Text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_id_display)
    if row.run_url:
        run_id = f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"
    run_state_text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_state_display)
    run_state_emoji = run_state_text.split(" ", 1)[0] if run_state_text.strip() else "-"

    # Build values list matching column order
    stage_display = strip_rich_markup(row.lifecycle_display)
    values: list[str | Text] = [
        plan_cell,
        stage_display,
        row.status_display,
        row.created_display,
        objective_cell,
        location_cell,
        row.pr_head_branch or row.worktree_branch or "-",
        run_id,
        run_state_emoji,
    ]
    values.append(row.author)

    checks_display = strip_rich_markup(row.checks_display)
    comments_display = strip_rich_markup(row.comments_display)
    if show_pr_column:
        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
        if row.pr_url:
            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
    else:
        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
    return tuple(values)

        erk dash -A                      # All users' plans (short form)
        erk dash --interval 10
        erk dash --label erk-plan --state open
        erk dash --limit 10
        erk dash --run-state in_progress
        erk dash --sort activity         # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    # Default to showing all columns (runs=True)
    prs = True  # Always show PRs
    runs = True  # Default to showing runs

    _run_interactive_mode(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        runs=runs,
        prs=prs,
        limit=limit,
        interval=interval,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Command to list plans with filtering."""

from collections.abc import Callable
from typing import ParamSpec, TypeVar

import click
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text

from erk.cli.commands.plan.output_builders import build_plan_list_entry
from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.cli.output.generic_renderer import render_json_list
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.display_utils import strip_rich_markup
from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir
from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
from erk.tui.data.types import PlanFilters, PlanRowData
from erk.tui.sorting.logic import sort_plans
from erk.tui.sorting.types import SortKey, SortState
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.fake import FakeBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.real import RealBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.fake import FakeClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.real import RealClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import get_pr_status_emoji
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, GitHubRepoLocation, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.http.auth import fetch_github_token
from erk_shared.gateway.http.fake import FakeHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.http.real import RealHttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.real import RealPlanDataProvider
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output

P = ParamSpec("P")
T = TypeVar("T")


def format_pr_cell(pr: PullRequestInfo, *, use_graphite: bool, graphite_url: str | None) -> str:
    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗

    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.

    Args:
        pr: PR information
        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)

    Returns:
        Formatted string for table cell with OSC 8 hyperlink
    """
    emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(pr)
    pr_text = f"#{pr.number}"

    # Append 🔗 for PRs that will close the issue when merged
    if pr.will_close_target:
        emoji += "🔗"

    # Determine which URL to use
    url = graphite_url if use_graphite else pr.url

    # Make PR number clickable if URL is available
    # Rich supports OSC 8 via [link=...] markup
    if url:
        return f"[link={url}]{pr_text}[/link] {emoji}"
    else:
        return f"{pr_text} {emoji}"


def plan_filter_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """Shared filter options for plan list commands."""
    f = click.option(
        "--label",
        multiple=True,
        help="Filter by label (can be specified multiple times for AND logic)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--state",
        type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"], case_sensitive=False),
        help="Filter by state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--run-state",
        type=click.Choice(
            ["queued", "in_progress", "success", "failure", "cancelled"], case_sensitive=False
        ),
        help="Filter by workflow run state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--limit",
        type=int,
        help="Maximum number of results to return",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--all-users",
        "-A",
        is_flag=True,
        default=False,
        help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--sort",
        type=click.Choice(["issue", "activity"], case_sensitive=False),
        default="issue",
        help="Sort order: by issue number (default) or recent branch activity",
    )(f)
    return f


def dash_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """TUI-specific options for dash command."""
    f = click.option(
        "--interval",
        type=float,
        default=15.0,
        help="Refresh interval in seconds (default: 15.0)",
    )(f)
    return f


def _build_static_table(
    rows: list[PlanRowData],
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> Table:
    """Build a Rich static table from PlanRowData rows.

    Column order matches the TUI PlanDataTable exactly.

    Args:
        rows: List of PlanRowData to display
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Rich Table populated with rows
    """
    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")

    # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns()
    table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6)
    table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4)
    table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7)
    table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5)
    table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42)
    table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9)
    if show_pr_column:
        table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5)

    for row in rows:
        values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column)
        table.add_row(*values)

    return table


def _row_to_static_values(
    row: PlanRowData,
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
    """Convert PlanRowData to static table cell values.

    Mirrors PlanDataTable._row_to_values() for Rich Console rendering.

    Args:
        row: Plan row data
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Tuple of cell values matching column order
    """
    # Format plan/PR number
    plan_cell: str | Text = f"#{row.plan_id}"
    if row.plan_url:
        plan_cell = f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"

    # Format objective cell
    objective_cell: str | Text = row.objective_display
    if row.objective_issue is not None and row.objective_url is not None:
        objective_cell = f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"
    elif row.objective_issue is not None:
        objective_cell = Text(row.objective_display, style="cyan")

    # Compact location emoji
    location_parts: list[str] = []
    if row.exists_locally:
        location_parts.append("\U0001f4bb")
    if row.run_url is not None:
        location_parts.append("\u2601")
    location_cell = "".join(location_parts) if location_parts else "-"

    # run-id and run-state
    run_id: str | Text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_id_display)
    if row.run_url:
        run_id = f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"
    run_state_text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_state_display)
    run_state_emoji = run_state_text.split(" ", 1)[0] if run_state_text.strip() else "-"

    # Build values list matching column order
    stage_display = strip_rich_markup(row.lifecycle_display)
    values: list[str | Text] = [
        plan_cell,
        stage_display,
        row.status_display,
        row.created_display,
        objective_cell,
        location_cell,
        row.pr_head_branch or row.worktree_branch or "-",
        run_id,
        run_state_emoji,
    ]
    values.append(row.author)

    checks_display = strip_rich_markup(row.checks_display)
    comments_display = strip_rich_markup(row.comments_display)
    if show_pr_column:
        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
        if row.pr_url:
            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
    else:
        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
    return tuple(values)


def _list_plans_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
    json_mode: bool,
) -> None:
    """Implementation logic for listing plans with optional filters.

    Uses RealPlanDataProvider to fetch data (same as erk dash TUI),
    then renders as a static Rich table matching the TUI column layout.
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Construct RealPlanDataProvider
    # Only fetch_plans() and fetch_branch_activity() are used here;
    # neither requires clipboard, browser, or http_client, so use fakes.
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=FakeClipboard(),
        browser=FakeBrowserLauncher(),
        http_client=FakeHttpClient(),
    )

    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=True,
        show_runs=True,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Fetch data via provider
    rows = provider.fetch_plans(filters)

    if not rows:
        user_output("No plans found matching the criteria.")
        return

    # Sort rows
    sort_key = SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID
    if sort_key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:
        activity_by_plan = provider.fetch_branch_activity(rows)
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key, activity_by_plan=activity_by_plan)
    else:
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key)

    # JSON output mode
    if json_mode:
        entries = [build_plan_list_entry(row) for row in rows]
        click.echo(render_json_list(entries))
        return

    # Build and display static table
    table = _build_static_table(
        rows,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    user_output(f"\nFound {len(rows)} plan(s):\n")
    console = Console(stderr=True, width=200, force_terminal=True)
    console.print(table)
    console.print()


def _run_interactive_mode(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    runs: bool,
    prs: bool,
    limit: int | None,
    interval: float,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Run interactive TUI mode.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
        label: Labels to filter by
        state: State filter ("open", "closed", or None)
        run_state: Workflow run state filter
        runs: Whether to show run columns
        prs: Whether to show PR columns
        limit: Maximum number of results
        interval: Refresh interval in seconds
        all_users: If True, show plans from all users; if False, filter to authenticated user
        sort: Sort order ("issue" or "activity")
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    # Get owner/repo from RepoContext (already populated via git remote URL parsing)
    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter: None for all users, authenticated username otherwise
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-plan",)

    # Create data provider and filters
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))
    clipboard = RealClipboard()
    browser = RealBrowserLauncher()

    # Fetch GitHub token once at startup for fast HTTP client
    token = fetch_github_token()
    http_client = RealHttpClient(token=token, base_url="https://api.github.com")

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=clipboard,
        browser=browser,
        http_client=http_client,
    )
    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=prs,
        show_runs=runs,
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    # Convert sort string to SortState
    initial_sort = SortState(key=SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID)

    # Run the TUI app
    app = ErkDashApp(
        provider=provider,
        filters=filters,
        refresh_interval=interval,
        initial_sort=initial_sort,
        plan_backend="planned_pr",
    )
    app.run()


@click.command("list")
@plan_filter_options
@click.pass_obj
def list_plans(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """List plans as a static table.

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Examples:
        erk plan list                    # Your plans only
        erk plan list --all-users        # All users' plans
        erk plan list -A                 # All users' plans (short form)
        erk plan list --state open
        erk plan list --label erk-plan --label bug
        erk plan list --limit 10
        erk plan list --run-state in_progress
        erk plan list --sort activity    # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    _list_plans_impl(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )


@click.command("dash")
@plan_filter_options
@dash_options
@click.pass_obj
def dash(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
    interval: float,
) -> None:
    """Interactive plan dashboard (TUI).

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Launches an interactive terminal UI for viewing and managing plans.
    Shows all columns (runs) by default. For a static table output, use
    'erk plan list' instead.

    Examples:
        erk dash                         # Your plans only
        erk dash --all-users             # All users' plans
        erk dash -A                      # All users' plans (short form)
        erk dash --interval 10
        erk dash --label erk-plan --state open
        erk dash --limit 10
        erk dash --run-state in_progress
        erk dash --sort activity         # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    # Default to showing all columns (runs=True)
    prs = True  # Always show PRs
    runs = True  # Default to showing runs

    _run_interactive_mode(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        runs=runs,
        prs=prs,
        limit=limit,
        interval=interval,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )

    42→def _format_value(value: object) -> str:
    43→    """Format a value for display, handling datetime conversion.
    44→
    45→    YAML parsing converts ISO 8601 timestamps to datetime objects.
    46→    This function converts them back to ISO format strings for display.
    47→
    48→    Args:
    49→        value: Any value from metadata
    50→
    51→    Returns:
    52→        String representation suitable for display
    53→    """
    54→    if isinstance(value, datetime):
    55→        return value.isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
    56→    return str(value)
    57→
    58→
    59→def _format_field(label: str, value: str) -> str:
    60→    """Format a field with dimmed label and consistent width.
    61→

    63→    plan view output fields.

    67→        value: The value to display
    68→
    69→    Returns:
    70→        Formatted string with styled label and value
    71→    """
    72→    label_width = 12
    73→    styled_label = click.style(f"{label}:".ljust(label_width), dim=True)
    74→    return f"{styled_label} {value}"
    75→
    76→
    77→def _format_learn_state(
    78→    learn_status: LearnStatusValue | None,
    79→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
    80→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
    81→) -> str:
    82→    """Format learn status for CLI display.
    83→
    84→    Args:
    85→        learn_status: Learn status value (see LearnStatusValue for valid values)
    86→        learn_plan_issue: Issue number of generated learn plan
    87→        learn_plan_pr: PR number that implemented the learn plan
    88→
    89→    Returns:
    90→        Formatted display string based on status
    91→    """
    92→    if learn_status is None or learn_status == "not_started":
    93→        return "- not started"
    94→    if learn_status == "pending":
    95→        return "in progress"
    96→    if learn_status == "completed_no_plan":
    97→        return "no insights"
    98→    if learn_status == "completed_with_plan" and learn_plan_issue is not None:
    99→        return f"#{learn_plan_issue}"
   100→    if learn_status == "pending_review" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   101→        return f"draft PR #{learn_plan_pr}"
   102→    if learn_status == "plan_completed" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   103→        return f"completed #{learn_plan_pr}"
   104→    return "- not started"
   105→

   107→def _format_header_section(header_info: dict[str, object], *, plan_url: str | None) -> list[str]:
   108→    """Format the header info section for display.
   109→
   110→    Args:
   111→        header_info: Dictionary of header fields from plan-header block
   112→        plan_url: GitHub issue URL for constructing workflow URLs
   113→
   114→    Returns:
   115→        List of formatted lines for display
   116→    """
   117→    lines: list[str] = []
   118→
   119→    # Skip if no header info
   120→    if not header_info:
   121→        return lines
   122→
   123→    lines.append("")
   124→    lines.append(click.style("─── Header ───", bold=True))
   125→
   126→    # Basic metadata
   127→    if CREATED_BY in header_info:
   128→        lines.append(_format_field("Created by", str(header_info[CREATED_BY])))
   129→
   130→    if SCHEMA_VERSION in header_info:
   131→        lines.append(_format_field("Schema version", str(header_info[SCHEMA_VERSION])))
   132→
   133→    if WORKTREE_NAME in header_info:
   134→        lines.append(_format_field("Worktree", str(header_info[WORKTREE_NAME])))
   135→
   136→    if OBJECTIVE_ISSUE in header_info:
   137→        lines.append(_format_field("Objective", f"#{header_info[OBJECTIVE_ISSUE]}"))
   138→
   139→    if SOURCE_REPO in header_info:
   140→        lines.append(_format_field("Source repo", str(header_info[SOURCE_REPO])))
   141→
   142→    # Local implementation info
   143→    has_local_impl = any(
   144→        k in header_info
   145→        for k in [LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_EVENT, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION]
   146→    )
   147→    if has_local_impl:
   148→        lines.append("")
   149→        lines.append(click.style("─── Local Implementation ───", bold=True))
   150→        if LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT in header_info:
   151→            event = header_info.get(LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_EVENT, "")
   152→            event_str = f" ({event})" if event else ""
   153→            timestamp = _format_value(header_info[LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT])
   154→            lines.append(_format_field("Last impl", f"{timestamp}{event_str}"))
   155→        if LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION in header_info:
   156→            lines.append(_format_field("Session", str(header_info[LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION])))
   157→        if LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_USER in header_info:
   158→            lines.append(_format_field("User", str(header_info[LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_USER])))
   159→
   160→    # Remote implementation info
   161→    if LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT in header_info:
   162→        lines.append("")
   163→        lines.append(click.style("─── Remote Implementation ───", bold=True))
   164→        lines.append(_format_field("Last impl", _format_value(header_info[LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT])))
   165→
   166→    # Remote dispatch info (GitHub Actions workflow triggers)
   167→    has_dispatch = LAST_DISPATCHED_AT in header_info or LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID in header_info
   168→    if has_dispatch:
   169→        lines.append("")
   170→        lines.append(click.style("─── Remote Dispatch ───", bold=True))
   171→        if LAST_DISPATCHED_AT in header_info:
   172→            lines.append(
   173→                _format_field("Last dispatched", _format_value(header_info[LAST_DISPATCHED_AT]))
   174→            )
   175→        if LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID in header_info:
   176→            lines.append(_format_field("Run ID", str(header_info[LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID])))
   177→
   178→    # Learn info - always show this section
   179→    lines.append("")
   180→    lines.append(click.style("─── Learn ───", bold=True))
   181→
   182→    # Display learn state (status, plan issue, PR)
   183→    learn_status_raw = header_info.get(LEARN_STATUS)
   184→    learn_plan_issue_raw = header_info.get(LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE)
   185→    learn_plan_pr_raw = header_info.get(LEARN_PLAN_PR)
   186→
   187→    # Cast to correct types (LBYL)
   188→    # Narrow learn_status to LearnStatusValue if valid
   189→    learn_status_str = learn_status_raw if isinstance(learn_status_raw, str) else None
   190→    learn_status_val = narrow_to_literal(learn_status_str, LearnStatusValue)
   191→
   192→    learn_plan_issue_int: int | None = None
   193→    if isinstance(learn_plan_issue_raw, int):
   194→        learn_plan_issue_int = learn_plan_issue_raw
   195→
   196→    learn_plan_pr_int: int | None = None
   197→    if isinstance(learn_plan_pr_raw, int):
   198→        learn_plan_pr_int = learn_plan_pr_raw
   199→
   200→    learn_display = _format_learn_state(learn_status_val, learn_plan_issue_int, learn_plan_pr_int)
   201→    lines.append(_format_field("Status", learn_display))
   202→
   203→    # Show workflow URL when learn is in progress
   204→    if learn_status_val == "pending":
   205→        learn_run_id_raw = header_info.get(LEARN_RUN_ID)
   206→        if learn_run_id_raw is not None and plan_url is not None:
   207→            owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
   208→            if owner_repo is not None:
   209→                workflow_url = construct_workflow_run_url(
   210→                    owner_repo[0], owner_repo[1], str(learn_run_id_raw)
   211→                )
   212→                lines.append(_format_field("Workflow", workflow_url))
   213→
   214→    if CREATED_FROM_SESSION in header_info:
   215→        lines.append(_format_field("Plan session", str(header_info[CREATED_FROM_SESSION])))
   216→    if LAST_LEARN_SESSION in header_info:
   217→        lines.append(_format_field("Learn session", str(header_info[LAST_LEARN_SESSION])))
   218→
   219→    return lines
   220→
   221→
   222→@click.command("view")
   223→@click.argument("identifier", type=str, required=False, default=None)
   224→@click.option("--full", "-f", is_flag=True, help="Show full plan body")
   225→@click.pass_obj
   226→def view_plan(ctx: ErkContext, identifier: str | None, *, full: bool) -> None:
   227→    """Fetch and display a plan by identifier.
   228→
   229→    IDENTIFIER can be a plain number (e.g., "42") or a GitHub issue URL
   230→    (e.g., "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123").
   231→
   232→    If not provided, infers the plan number from the current branch name
   233→    (e.g., branch "P123-foo-bar" will view plan #123).
   234→
   235→    By default, shows only header information. Use --full to display
   236→    the complete plan body.
   237→    """
   238→    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
   239→    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)  # Ensure erk metadata directories exist
   240→    repo_root = repo.root  # Use git repository root for GitHub operations
   241→
   242→    # Resolve plan: explicit argument or infer from branch
   243→    if identifier is not None:
   244→        issue_number = parse_issue_identifier(identifier)
   245→        if issue_number is None:
   246→            user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Invalid identifier: {identifier}")
   247→            raise SystemExit(1)
   248→        result = ctx.plan_store.get_plan(repo_root, str(issue_number))
   249→        plan_id = str(issue_number)
   250→    else:
   251→        # Try to infer from current branch
   252→        branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.cwd)
   253→        if branch is None:
   254→            user_output(
   255→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   256→                + "No identifier specified and could not infer from branch name"
   257→            )
   258→            user_output("Usage: erk plan view <identifier>")
   259→            user_output("Or run from a branch named P{issue}-...")
   260→            raise SystemExit(1)
   261→        result = ctx.plan_backend.get_plan_for_branch(repo_root, branch)
   262→        plan_id = ctx.plan_backend.resolve_plan_id_for_branch(repo_root, branch)
   263→        if plan_id is None:
   264→            user_output(
   265→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   266→                + "No identifier specified and could not infer from branch name"
   267→            )
   268→            user_output("Usage: erk plan view <identifier>")
   269→            user_output("Or run from a branch named P{issue}-...")
   270→            raise SystemExit(1)
   271→
   272→    if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound):
   273→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Issue #{plan_id} not found")
   274→        raise SystemExit(1)
   275→    plan = result
   276→
   277→    # Extract header info via plan_backend for branch display and later header section
   278→    all_meta = ctx.plan_backend.get_all_metadata_fields(repo_root, plan_id)
   279→    if isinstance(all_meta, PlanNotFound):
   280→        header_info: dict[str, object] = {}
   281→    else:
   282→        header_info = all_meta
   283→
   284→    # Display plan details with consistent formatting
   285→    user_output("")
   286→    user_output(_format_field("Title", click.style(plan.title, bold=True)))
   287→
   288→    # Display metadata with clickable ID
   289→    state_color = "green" if plan.state.value == "OPEN" else "red"
   290→    user_output(_format_field("State", click.style(plan.state.value, fg=state_color)))
   291→
   292→    # Make ID clickable using OSC 8 if URL is available
   293→    id_text = f"#{plan_id}"
   294→    if plan.url:
   295→        colored_id = click.style(id_text, fg="cyan")
   296→        clickable_id = f"\033]8;;{plan.url}\033\\{colored_id}\033]8;;\033\\"
   297→    else:
   298→        clickable_id = click.style(id_text, fg="cyan")
   299→    user_output(_format_field("ID", clickable_id))
   300→    user_output(_format_field("URL", plan.url or "-"))
   301→
   302→    # Display branch if available from plan-header
   303→    branch_name = header_info.get(BRANCH_NAME)
   304→    if branch_name:
   305→        user_output(_format_field("Branch", str(branch_name)))
   306→
   307→    # Display labels
   308→    if plan.labels:
   309→        labels_str = ", ".join(
   310→            click.style(f"[{label}]", fg="bright_magenta") for label in plan.labels
   311→        )
   312→        user_output(_format_field("Labels", labels_str))
   313→
   314→    # Display assignees
   315→    if plan.assignees:
   316→        assignees_str = ", ".join(plan.assignees)
   317→        user_output(_format_field("Assignees", assignees_str))
   318→
   319→    # Display timestamps
   320→    created = plan.created_at.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")
   321→    updated = plan.updated_at.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")
   322→    user_output(_format_field("Created", created))
   323→    user_output(_format_field("Updated", updated))
   324→
   325→    # Display header info section
   326→    header_lines = _format_header_section(header_info, plan_url=plan.url)
   327→    for line in header_lines:
   328→        user_output(line)
   329→
   330→    # Display body only with --full flag
   331→    if full and plan.body:
   332→        user_output("")
   333→        user_output(click.style("─── Plan ───", bold=True))
   334→        user_output(plan.body)
